comments.text: I computed $f(N)$ for $1 \le N \le 250$. Method: The vertices are odd squarefree integers $a \le N$ (even or non-squarefree $a$ fail since $2a$ is not squarefree), with an edge between distinct $a,b$ if $a+b$ is not squarefree. Then $f(N)$ is the maximum independent set size. I used branch-and-bound with bitsets, cross-checked against brute-force for small $N$. $$\begin{aligned} 1 &\le N \le 4 &:\qquad f(N) &= 1\\ 5 &\le N \le 18 &:\qquad f(N) &= 2\\ 19 &\le N \le 22 &:\qquad f(N) &= 3\\ 23 &\le N \le 36 &:\qquad f(N) &= 4\\ 37 &\le N \le 40 &:\qquad f(N) &= 5\\ 41 &\le N \le 58 &:\qquad f(N) &= 6\\ 59 &\le N \le 86 &:\qquad f(N) &= 7\\ 87 &\le N \le 100 &:\qquad f(N) &= 8\\ 101 &\le N \le 104 &:\qquad f(N) &= 9\\ 105 &\le N \le 112 &:\qquad f(N) &= 10\\ 113 &\le N \le 130 &:\qquad f(N) &= 11\\ 131 &\le N \le 150 &:\qquad f(N) &= 12\\ 151 &\le N \le 158 &:\qquad f(N) &= 13\\ 159 &\le N \le 166 &:\qquad f(N) &= 14\\ 167 &\le N \le 194 &:\qquad f(N) &= 15\\ 195 &\le N \le 202 &:\qquad f(N) &= 16\\ 203 &\le N \le 238 &:\qquad f(N) &= 17\\ 239 &\le N \le 250 &:\qquad f(N) &= 18 \end{aligned}$$ Example: $f(101) = 9$ is achieved by $\{5, 17, 29, 41, 53, 65, 77, 89, 101\}$, an arithmetic progression with common difference 12. All pairwise sums have the form $10 + 12k$, which is squarefree for $k \le 16$. The sequence of transition points $(1, 5, 19, 23, 37, 41, 59, 87, 101, 105, 113, 131, 151, 159, 167, 195, 203, 239, \ldots)$ does not appear in OEIS.
This latter sequence looks suitable for submission to the OEIS to me.
